qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) for faculty members and their research groups in Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) enables faculty members and their research groups to access compute, storage, and cloud resources beyond what can be obtained via the Rapid Access Service (RAS).

qa_pairs.question: What are the key application and submission deadlines for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) in 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications for Resources for Research Groups (RRG) and Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) open on September 23, 2025, and are due by November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time, with no extensions possible.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main application processes available within the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main application processes are "Resources for Research Groups (RRG)" and "Research Platforms and Portals (RPP)".

qa_pairs.question: What differentiates the "Resources for Research Groups (RRG)" process from the "Research Platforms and Portals (RPP)" process in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: RRG is for projects primarily conducting research requiring compute, storage, and cloud resources, while RPP is for projects primarily providing a service through scientific gateways, enhancing online tools, or advancing collaborations.

qa_pairs.question: What are the eligibility requirements for a Principal Investigator (PI) to submit an application to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI and all Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and have an active Alliance account in the CCDB with an Academic PI role (Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian).

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum number of Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications a Principal Investigator (PI) can submit to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) at one time?
qa_pairs.answer: A Principal Investigator (PI) can apply as PI for only one RRG application at a time, either through the full application or the Fast Track process.

qa_pairs.question: What are the minimum resource thresholds for CPU and GPU requests in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) on individual clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: A CPU request must be equal to or greater than 200 core-years or core-equivalent-years in each cluster, and a GPU request must be equal to or greater than 25 Reference GPU units (RGU) years in each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What are the minimum storage requirements for Project Storage and Nearline Storage to be eligible for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application on General Purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The minimum for Project Storage is 41 TB, and for Nearline Storage is 101 TB. These are the sums of all the project or nearline storage needed across all General Purpose clusters (Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval).

qa_pairs.question: What is the page limit for the main application document in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications, before references, must not exceed 10 pages. References can be submitted separately if the total application exceeds 10 pages.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Canadian Common CV (CCV) requirement for Principal Investigators and Co-PIs in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI and all Co-PIs are required to upload an up-to-date CCV with any RAC application, and failing to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the application's overall score.

qa_pairs.question: What are pre-submission consultations for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) and who is encouraged to attend them?
qa_pairs.answer: Pre-submission consultations help determine if a project justifies a RAC application, verify eligibility for the correct process, and provide technical assistance with resource calculations. First-time applicants are strongly encouraged to attend before October 31, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What happens during the technical review phase of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) assessment process?
qa_pairs.answer: Technical experts ensure appropriate systems are requested, evaluate application efficiency and scalability, identify optimization needs, flag discrepancies between requested resources, and provide a technical opinion on the reasonability of the request.

qa_pairs.question: What is the expected response time for applicants when the technical team requests additional information during the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) technical review?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants are expected to respond to requests from the technical team within 48 hours to ensure their application can progress beyond the technical review.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main evaluation criteria for Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), and what are their respective weights?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main criteria are "Research Methods" (70%) and "Resource Management and Computational Expertise" (30%).

qa_pairs.question: What score is considered unsuccessful for an application submitted to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications with a score of 2.0 or lower on the 5-point scale are considered unsuccessful and will not be awarded resources.

qa_pairs.question: What constitutes a direct conflict of interest for a peer reviewer in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Direct conflicts include submitting an application to the same committee, being from the same university department, having been a research supervisor or graduate student of the applicant within the past five years, collaborating or publishing with the applicant within the past two years, or having a personal relationship or financial stake.

qa_pairs.question: What should an applicant do if they are unsure which Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application process (RRG or RPP) is best suited for their project?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants should email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca for guidance.

qa_pairs.question: If a project needs both project and nearline storage but only one of the requests meets its minimum threshold for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), can both be requested?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if one of the storage requests (project or nearline) is equal to or greater than its specified minimum, then both resources can be requested and must be included in the online form.
